lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The radio crackles before the board updates. Three vehicles. Black ice on the interchange. First call comes in at 02:14, and by 02:17 the overnight team already knows this night has changed its mind about being manageable.

lines.speaker: Theo
lines.text: All right, listen up. Multi-vehicle on the 9 interchange. Three confirmed critical, seven walking wounded on scene. First rig inbound, two minutes. Maeve, you're lead on Bay One. Reza, Bay Three. I want IVs primed and the crash cart unlocked before that bay door opens.

lines.speaker: Reza
lines.text: Already on it.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The bay doors open on a gust of February that cuts straight through the fluorescents. And then the wheels of the first stretcher hit the floor — hard, fast, the paramedic at the head running it in without breaking stride.

lines.speaker: Dana
lines.text: GCS seven on scene, dropped to five in transport. Male, mid-forties, blunt chest trauma, tension pneumo right side, we decompressed in the rig but pressure's already re-building. BP's sixty palp. Two large-bore IVs, one blew on the ramp.

lines.speaker: Theo
lines.text: Bay One. Maeve —

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Maeve is already there. She was already moving before Theo said her name, the way you move when something in your body remembers a frequency before your mind catches up. She takes the foot of the stretcher. Her eyes go to the patient first — they have to, they always do — and then, only then, to the paramedic at the head.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Dana Voss. Standing there in a high-vis jacket with road salt on the knees and a smear of something dark across her left forearm. Looking back.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: One second. Maybe less. Then both of them turn to the man dying between them and that second closes over like ice.

lines.speaker: Maeve
lines.text: Tension pneumo confirmed, I need a chest dart. Someone call respiratory. And get me ultrasound, I want to see that pericardium before we do anything else.

lines.speaker: Dana
lines.text: He was talking on scene. Asking about his dog.

lines.speaker: Maeve
lines.style: quiet, to herself as much as Dana
lines.text: Okay. Good. That's good.

lines.speaker: Reza
lines.text: Ultrasound's coming from Three —

lines.speaker: Theo
lines.text: Leave it, I've got one here. Reza, your patient is awake and asking for you.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Theo positions the probe. Maeve reads the image without being told what she's looking at. There's fluid in the pericardial sac — not a flood, not yet, but a tide coming in.

lines.speaker: Maeve
lines.text: Tamponade developing. We're not waiting for CT. I need a thoracic tray and I need the attending paged. Now.

lines.speaker: Dana
lines.text: His pressure's holding at sixty-two. It held longer in the rig than I expected.

lines.speaker: Maeve
lines.text: How long was your transport?

lines.speaker: Dana
lines.text: Eleven minutes door to door. Scene was — complicated.

lines.speaker: Maeve
lines.text: You decompressed blind?

lines.speaker: Dana
lines.text: Needle in the dark at sixty miles an hour on a county road. Second line, second try. Yeah.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Something passes across Maeve's face. Not approval exactly. Recognition. She has seen Dana work in the dark before, in different weather, in a different country, and she knows what that kind of steadiness costs.

lines.speaker: Maeve
lines.text: Hold his head. Talk to him. I need him as calm as we can keep him while I prep this.

lines.speaker: Dana
lines.text: Hey. Hey, sir — I'm Dana, I've been with you since the interchange. You're inside now. You're in the hospital. The people here are very good.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He doesn't respond. But his fingers move. Dana notices. She keeps her hand where it is.

lines.speaker: Theo
lines.text: Attending's two minutes out. Pressure?

lines.speaker: Maeve
lines.text: Coming up slightly. Sixty-eight. Dana, if he drops again —

lines.speaker: Dana
lines.text: I'll call it.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Not Doctor Callahan. Not Maeve. Just — I'll call it. The kind of sentence two people finish for each other after enough shared floors. Reza glances over from Bay Three. Theo does not look up from the monitor, but his jaw shifts slightly, and he files the thing he heard away for later.

lines.speaker: Maeve
lines.text: Okay. We're going to move him to the procedure bay. On my count. One —

lines.speaker: Dana
lines.text: Two.

lines.speaker: Maeve
lines.text: Three.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: They move the man together through the fluorescent noise of the ER floor, and for forty feet neither of them says another word about Cork, or the three years between, or whatever it was that ended. There is only the patient between them, and the specific gravity of a shared past that neither of them has put down.
